steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The passage explains that House districts average 760,000 people, then states Alaska has only 730,000 — a cause. The blank introduces the effect: Alaska has just one district covering the whole state. The transition must signal a cause-and-effect relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'As a result,': signals that what follows is a consequence of what came before. Alaska's population falling below the district average directly causes it to have only one district. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Instead,': signals contrast or substitution — it would imply Alaska does something different rather than because of its population size. The sentence is not presenting an alternative; it is presenting an outcome. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Finally,': signals the last item in a sequence or a concluding step in a process. The passage is not listing a series of steps; it is explaining a consequence of Alaska's population. ✗
  • Choice D — 'For instance,': signals that what follows is an example of a general claim. Alaska's one-district situation is the result of its population size, not merely an illustration of the average-size-district point. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
